# File Summary

## Purpose:

This file contains a packed representation of the entire repository's contents.
It is designed to be easily consumable by AI systems for analysis, code review,
or other automated processes.

## File Format:

The content is organized as follows:
1. This summary section
2. Repository information
3. Repository structure
4. Multiple file entries, each consisting of:
   a. A header with the file path (## File: path/to/file)
   b. The full contents of the file in a code block

## Usage Guidelines:

- This file should be treated as read-only. Any changes should be made to the
  original repository files, not this packed version.
- When processing this file, use the file path to distinguish
  between different files in the repository.
- Be aware that this file may contain sensitive information. Handle it with
  the same level of security as you would the original repository.

## Notes:

- Some files may have been excluded based on .gitignore rules and Repomix's
  configuration.
- Binary files are not included in this packed representation. Please refer to
  the Repository Structure section for a complete list of file paths, including
  binary files.

## behavioural.drl

```text
package com.github.cetkovicaleksa.droolmate.rules;
import com.github.cetkovicaleksa.droolmate.model.Breed;
import com.github.cetkovicaleksa.droolmate.model.RoleRequirement;
import com.github.cetkovicaleksa.droolmate.model.PhysicalScore;
import com.github.cetkovicaleksa.droolmate.model.BehavioralScore;

dialect "mvel"

/**
 * Layer 2: Behavioral Trait Evaluation
 *
 * These rules assess whether a breed's behavioral characteristics
 * match the psychological and emotional requirements of a role.
 * Depends on PhysicalScore facts from Layer 1.
 *
 * @source: Hart (1985) - Behavioral profiles of dog breeds using cluster analysis
 * Three factors: Reactivity (tenderness/protectiveness), Aggression (dominance),
 * Trainability (obedience). These factors explain 81% of behavioral variance across breeds.
 *
 * @source: Handbook on Animal-Assisted Therapy - behavioral requirements for roles
 */

rule "Behavioral evaluation start marker"
    agenda-group "behavioral"
    salience 20
    when
        $physicalScore : PhysicalScore()
    then
        System.out.println("=== Layer 2: Behavioral Evaluation Starting ===");
end

// Rule: Emit combined behavioral score
rule "Emit behavioral score for breed-role pair"
    agenda-group "behavioral"
    salience 15
    when
        $breed : Breed()
        $role : RoleRequirement()
        $physicalScore : PhysicalScore(breedName == $breed.name, roleId == $role.roleId)
    then
        // Hart (1985) factors
        double reactivityScore = $breed.getReactivityScore();
        double aggressionScore = $breed.getAggressionScore();
        double trainabilityScore = $breed.getTrainabilityScore();

        // Calculate individual factor fits
        double minReactivity = $role.getMinReactivityScore();
        double maxReactivity = $role.getMaxReactivityScore();
        double maxAggression = $role.getMaxAggressionScore();
        double minTrainability = $role.getMinTrainabilityScore();

        // Reactivity fit: 0-100
        double roleReactivityFit;
        if (reactivityScore >= minReactivity && reactivityScore <= maxReactivity) {
            roleReactivityFit = 100;
        } else {
            roleReactivityFit = Math.max(0, 100 - (Math.abs(reactivityScore - (minReactivity + maxReactivity) / 2) * 10));
        }

        // Aggression fit: 0-100 (lower aggression is better)
        double roleAggressionFit;
        if (aggressionScore <= maxAggression) {
            roleAggressionFit = 100;
        } else {
            roleAggressionFit = Math.max(0, 100 - ((aggressionScore - maxAggression) * 15));
        }

        // Trainability fit: 0-100 (higher trainability is better)
        double roleTrainabilityFit;
        if (trainabilityScore >= minTrainability) {
            roleTrainabilityFit = 100 - ((10 - trainabilityScore) * 5);
        } else {
            roleTrainabilityFit = Math.max(0, (trainabilityScore / minTrainability) * 100);
        }

        // Combined behavioral score (weighted average)
        // Weighting: Trainability 50%, Aggression 30%, Reactivity 20%
        double behavioralScore = (roleTrainabilityFit * 0.5) +
                                (roleAggressionFit * 0.3) +
                                (roleReactivityFit * 0.2);
        behavioralScore = Math.min(100, Math.max(0, behavioralScore));

        String reasoning = "Hart (1985) factors - Reactivity: " + String.format("%.1f", roleReactivityFit) + "%, " +
                          "Aggression: " + String.format("%.1f", roleAggressionFit) + "%, " +
                          "Trainability: " + String.format("%.1f", roleTrainabilityFit) + "%";

        BehavioralScore score = new BehavioralScore(
            $breed.getName(),
            $role.getRoleId(),
            reactivityScore,
            aggressionScore,
            trainabilityScore,
            roleReactivityFit,
            roleAggressionFit,
            roleTrainabilityFit,
            behavioralScore,
            reasoning
        );

        insert(score);
        System.out.println("[Behavioral] Score emitted: " + $breed.getName() + " → " + $role.getRoleName() +
                          " = " + String.format("%.1f", behavioralScore) + " (R:" + String.format("%.0f", roleReactivityFit) +
                          " A:" + String.format("%.0f", roleAggressionFit) + " T:" + String.format("%.0f", roleTrainabilityFit) + ")");
end
```

## compatibility.drl

```text
package com.github.cetkovicaleksa.droolmate.rules;
import com.github.cetkovicaleksa.droolmate.model.Breed;
import com.github.cetkovicaleksa.droolmate.model.RoleRequirement;
import com.github.cetkovicaleksa.droolmate.model.PhysicalScore;
import com.github.cetkovicaleksa.droolmate.model.BehavioralScore;
import com.github.cetkovicaleksa.droolmate.model.Recommendation;
import java.util.List;
import java.util.ArrayList;

dialect "mvel"

/**
 * Layer 3: Compatibility Ranking and Final Recommendation
 *
 * These rules combine physical and behavioral scores to produce
 * final recommendations. Uses accumulate() to rank candidates.
 * Depends on BehavioralScore facts from Layer 2.
 *
 * @source: Hart (1985) + ADI Standards - combined assessment framework
 * @source: Handbook on Animal-Assisted Therapy - ADI criteria for service animal suitability
 */

rule "Compatibility evaluation start marker"
    agenda-group "compatibility"
    salience 20
    when
        $behavioralScore : BehavioralScore()
    then
        System.out.println("=== Layer 3: Compatibility and Ranking ===");
end

// Rule: Generate recommendation with combined scoring
rule "Generate breed recommendation"
    agenda-group "compatibility"
    salience 15
    when
        $breed : Breed()
        $role : RoleRequirement()
        $physicalScore : PhysicalScore(breedName == $breed.name, roleId == $role.roleId)
        $behavioralScore : BehavioralScore(breedName == $breed.name, roleId == $role.roleId)
    then
        // Combined score: Physical 30% + Behavioral 70%
        // Behavioral weighted higher as it's more critical for successful service
        double combinedScore = ($physicalScore.getPhysicalScore() * 0.3) +
                              ($behavioralScore.getBehavioralScore() * 0.7);
        combinedScore = Math.min(100, Math.max(0, combinedScore));

        // Determine recommendation level
        // Minimum thresholds (ADI standards):
        // - Physical: must be >= 50 (at least one dimension suitable)
        // - Behavioral: must be >= 75 (high behavioral compatibility)
        // - Combined: >= 75 for recommendation, >= 85 for excellent

        boolean physicalOk = $physicalScore.getPhysicalScore() >= 50;
        boolean behavioralOk = $behavioralScore.getBehavioralScore() >= 75;
        boolean combinedOk = combinedScore >= 75;

        boolean meetsMinimumThreshold = physicalOk && behavioralOk && combinedOk;

        String recommendationLevel;
        if (combinedScore >= 85) {
            recommendationLevel = "Excellent";
        } else if (combinedScore >= 75) {
            recommendationLevel = "Good";
        } else if (combinedScore >= 60) {
            recommendationLevel = "Fair";
        } else {
            recommendationLevel = "Not Recommended";
        }

        String reasoning =
            "Physical: " + String.format("%.1f", $physicalScore.getPhysicalScore()) + "% | " +
            "Behavioral: " + String.format("%.1f", $behavioralScore.getBehavioralScore()) + "% | " +
            "Combined: " + String.format("%.1f", combinedScore) + "% | " +
            "Level: " + recommendationLevel;

        Recommendation recommendation = new Recommendation(
            $breed.getName(),
            $role.getRoleId(),
            $role.getRoleName(),
            $physicalScore.getPhysicalScore(),
            $behavioralScore.getBehavioralScore(),
            combinedScore,
            meetsMinimumThreshold,
            recommendationLevel,
            reasoning
        );

        insert(recommendation);

        String recommendedStr;
        if (meetsMinimumThreshold) {
            recommendedStr = "RECOMMENDED";
        } else {
            recommendedStr = "NOT RECOMMENDED";
        }

        System.out.println("[Compatibility] " + recommendedStr + ": " + $breed.getName() +
                          " for " + $role.getRoleName() + " = " + String.format("%.1f", combinedScore) +
                          "% (" + recommendationLevel + ")");
end

// Rule: Rank and filter recommendations
// This accumulates all recommendations for a given role and ranks them
rule "Rank breed recommendations for role"
    agenda-group "compatibility"
    salience 10
    when
        $role : RoleRequirement()
        $recommendations : List(size > 0) from accumulate(
            $rec : Recommendation(roleId == $role.roleId),
            init(List list = new java.util.ArrayList()),
            action(list.add($rec)),
            result(list)
        )
    then
        // Sort recommendations by combined score (descending)
        // Using stream API for cleaner sorting
        java.util.stream.Stream stream = $recommendations.stream();
        java.util.List sorted = stream.sorted((r1, r2) -> {
            Recommendation rec1 = (Recommendation) r1;
            Recommendation rec2 = (Recommendation) r2;
            return Double.compare(rec2.getCombinedScore(), rec1.getCombinedScore());
        }).collect(java.util.stream.Collectors.toList());

        System.out.println("\n=== FINAL RANKINGS FOR ROLE: " + $role.getRoleName() + " ===");
        for (int i = 0; i < sorted.size(); i++) {
            Recommendation rec = (Recommendation) sorted.get(i);
            String status = rec.isRecommended() ? "✓" : "✗";
            System.out.println((i+1) + ". " + status + " " + rec.getBreedName() +
                              " - " + String.format("%.1f%%", rec.getCombinedScore()) +
                              " (" + rec.getRecommendationLevel() + ")");
        }
        System.out.println("=== END RANKINGS ===\n");
end
```

## template/role_requirement.drt

```text
package com.github.cetkovicaleksa.droolmate.rules;
dialect "mvel"

# Template for generating role requirement rules
# Each row defines criteria for a specific role
# @source: ADI Standards and Ethics

template "Role Requirement Template"

rule "Initialize Role Requirement @{roleName}"
    when
    then
        # Create and insert role requirement fact
        RoleRequirement requirement = new RoleRequirement(
            "@{roleId}",                  # roleId
            "@{roleName}",                # roleName
            @{minHeightCm},              # minHeightCm
            @{maxHeightCm},              # maxHeightCm
            @{minWeightKg},              # minWeightKg
            @{maxWeightKg},              # maxWeightKg
            @{minReactivityScore},       # minReactivityScore (lower = less reactive)
            @{maxReactivityScore},       # maxReactivityScore
            @{maxAggressionScore},       # maxAggressionScore (must be low)
            @{minTrainabilityScore},     # minTrainabilityScore (must be high)
            "@{description}"             # description
        );
        insert(requirement);
end

end template
```

## template/breed_initialization.drt

```text
package com.github.cetkovicaleksa.droolmate.rules;
dialect "mvel"

# Template for generating breed fact rules from CSV
# Each row in the CSV generates a rule that asserts a Breed fact
# @source: FCI Breed Standards

template "Breed Template"

rule "Initialize Breed @{name}"
    when
    then
        # Create and insert breed fact with characteristics from CSV
        Breed breed = new Breed(
            "@{name}",                    # breedName
            "@{fciGroup}",                # fciGroup
            @{minHeightCm},              # minHeightCm
            @{maxHeightCm},              # maxHeightCm
            @{minWeightKg},              # minWeightKg
            @{maxWeightKg},              # maxWeightKg
            @{reactivityScore},          # reactivityScore (Hart 1985)
            @{aggressionScore},          # aggressionScore (Hart 1985)
            @{trainabilityScore},        # trainabilityScore (Hart 1985)
            "@{description}"             # description
        );
        insert(breed);
end

end template
```

package com.github.cetkovicaleksa.droolmate.rules;
dialect "mvel"

# Template for generating breed fact rules from CSV
# Each row in the CSV generates a rule that asserts a Breed fact
# @source: FCI Breed Standards

template "Breed Template"

rule "Initialize Breed @{name}"
    agenda-group "initialization"
    when
    then
        # Create and insert breed fact with characteristics from CSV
        Breed breed = new Breed(
            "@{name}",                    # breedName
            "@{fciGroup}",                # fciGroup
            @{minHeightCm},              # minHeightCm
            @{maxHeightCm},              # maxHeightCm
            @{minWeightKg},              # minWeightKg
            @{maxWeightKg},              # maxWeightKg
            @{reactivityScore},          # reactivityScore (Hart 1985)
            @{aggressionScore},          # aggressionScore (Hart 1985)
            @{trainabilityScore},        # trainabilityScore (Hart 1985)
            "@{description}"             # description
        );
        insert(breed);
end

end template

## physical.drl

```text
package com.github.cetkovicaleksa.droolmate.rules;
import com.github.cetkovicaleksa.droolmate.model.Breed;
import com.github.cetkovicaleksa.droolmate.model.RoleRequirement;
import com.github.cetkovicaleksa.droolmate.model.PhysicalScore;

dialect "mvel"

/**
 * Layer 1: Physical Trait Evaluation
 *
 * These rules assess whether a breed's physical characteristics
 * (height and weight) are suitable for a given role.
 *
 * @source: FCI Breed Standards - physical characteristic definitions
 * @source: Handbook on Animal-Assisted Therapy - physical requirements for roles
 */

// Initialization - ensure breed facts are inserted first
rule "Breed initialization marker"
    agenda-group "initialization"
    when
    then
        System.out.println("=== Layer 1: Physical Evaluation Starting ===");
end

// Rule: Create PhysicalScore fact combining height and weight evaluation
rule "Emit physical score for breed-role pair"
    agenda-group "physical"
    salience 10
    when
        $breed : Breed()
        $role : RoleRequirement()
    then
        double breedAvgHeight = ($breed.getMinHeightCm() + $breed.getMaxHeightCm()) / 2;
        double breedAvgWeight = ($breed.getMinWeightKg() + $breed.getMaxWeightKg()) / 2;

        boolean heightSuitable =
            breedAvgHeight >= $role.getMinHeightCm() &&
            breedAvgHeight <= $role.getMaxHeightCm();

        boolean weightSuitable =
            breedAvgWeight >= $role.getMinWeightKg() &&
            breedAvgWeight <= $role.getMaxWeightKg();

        // Physical score: 100 if both suitable, 50 if one suitable, 0 if none
        double physicalScore = 0;
        if (heightSuitable && weightSuitable) {
            physicalScore = 100;
        } else if (heightSuitable || weightSuitable) {
            physicalScore = 50;
        }

        String heightSymbol;
        if (heightSuitable) {
            heightSymbol = "✓";
        } else {
            heightSymbol = "✗";
        }

        String weightSymbol;
        if (weightSuitable) {
            weightSymbol = "✓";
        } else {
            weightSymbol = "✗";
        }

        String reasoning = "Physical match: Height " + heightSymbol + ", Weight " + weightSymbol;

        String heightStr;
        if (heightSuitable) {
            heightStr = "Height ✓: " + String.format("%.1f cm", breedAvgHeight);
        } else {
            heightStr = "Height ✗: " + String.format("%.1f cm", breedAvgHeight);
        }

        String weightStr;
        if (weightSuitable) {
            weightStr = "Weight ✓: " + String.format("%.1f kg", breedAvgWeight);
        } else {
            weightStr = "Weight ✗: " + String.format("%.1f kg", breedAvgWeight);
        }

        PhysicalScore score = new PhysicalScore(
            $breed.getName(),
            $role.getRoleId(),
            heightSuitable,
            weightSuitable,
            physicalScore,
            reasoning
        );

        insert(score);
        System.out.println("[Physical] Score emitted: " + $breed.getName() + " → " + $role.getRoleName() +
                          " = " + physicalScore + " (" + heightStr + ", " + weightStr + ")");
end
```

## backward.drl

```text
package com.github.cetkovicaleksa.droolmate.rules;

import com.github.cetkovicaleksa.droolmate.model.*;

dialect "mvel"

/**
 * Backward-style (goal-driven) coordination rules.
 *
 * These rules implement a goal decomposition pattern:
 * - When a `RoleQuery` (user goal) is asserted, create per-breed `ComputeBreedGoal` (PHYSICAL stage).
 * - When a PHYSICAL goal has its `PhysicalScore` available, emit a BEHAVIORAL goal for that breed-role.
 * - When a BEHAVIORAL goal has its `BehavioralScore` available, emit a COMPATIBILITY goal.
 * - When a COMPATIBILITY goal exists, compatibility rules will produce `Recommendation` facts.
 *
 * This simulates backward chaining by decomposing a top-level goal into subgoals and advancing them
 * as preconditions (lower-level facts) become available. The approach preserves traceability (goals
 * remain in working memory) and allows recursive hierarchies if `RoleRequirement` has parentRoleId.
 */

rule "Start backward reasoning for role query"
    when
        $q : RoleQuery()
        $role : RoleRequirement(roleId == $q.getRoleId())
    then
        // For every breed in the knowledge base create a physical computation goal
        System.out.println("[Backward] Received RoleQuery for role=" + $q.getRoleId() + " -> creating ComputeBreedGoal for all breeds");
        for (Object o : drools.getKieRuntime().getObjects()) {
            if (o instanceof Breed) {
                Breed b = (Breed) o;
                // create PHYSICAL stage goal for this breed-role pair
                ComputeBreedGoal g = new ComputeBreedGoal(b.getName(), $role.getRoleId(), GoalStage.PHYSICAL);
                insert(g);
            }
        }
end

rule "Advance PHYSICAL goal to BEHAVIORAL when PhysicalScore available"
    when
        $g : ComputeBreedGoal(isPhysical == true)
        $ps : PhysicalScore(breedName == $g.getBreedName(), roleId == $g.getRoleId())
    then
        // create next stage goal
        ComputeBreedGoal next = new ComputeBreedGoal($g.getBreedName(), $g.getRoleId(), GoalStage.BEHAVIORAL);
        insert(next);
        System.out.println("[Backward] PHYSICAL goal satisfied for " + $g.getBreedName() + " -> inserted BEHAVIORAL goal");
end

rule "Advance BEHAVIORAL goal to COMPATIBILITY when BehavioralScore available"
    when
        $g : ComputeBreedGoal(isBehavioral == true)
        $bs : BehavioralScore(breedName == $g.getBreedName(), roleId == $g.getRoleId())
    then
        ComputeBreedGoal next = new ComputeBreedGoal($g.getBreedName(), $g.getRoleId(), GoalStage.COMPATIBILITY);
        insert(next);
        System.out.println("[Backward] BEHAVIORAL goal satisfied for " + $g.getBreedName() + " -> inserted COMPATIBILITY goal");
end

rule "Trigger compatibility when COMPATIBILITY goal present and no Recommendation yet"
    when
        $g : ComputeBreedGoal(isCompatibility == true)
        not Recommendation(breedName == $g.getBreedName(), roleId == $g.getRoleId())
    then
        // Compatibility rules will generate Recommendation when PhysicalScore and BehavioralScore exist.
        System.out.println("[Backward] COMPATIBILITY goal exists for " + $g.getBreedName() + ", awaiting Recommendation creation.");
end

// If a RoleRequirement references a parent role, create a new RoleQuery for parent to recursively resolve inherited requirements
rule "Propagate role query to parent role"
    when
        $q : RoleQuery()
        $role : RoleRequirement(roleId == $q.getRoleId(), parentRoleId != null)
        $parent : RoleRequirement(roleId == $role.getParentRoleId())
    then
        // create a parent RoleQuery to ensure parent's constraints are considered
        RoleQuery pq = new RoleQuery($parent.getRoleId());
        insert(pq);
        System.out.println("[Backward] Role " + $role.getRoleId() + " has parent " + $parent.getRoleId() + ". Created parent RoleQuery.");
end

// Clean-up helper: when a Recommendation is created for breed-role, remove outstanding goals for that pair
rule "Cleanup goals after recommendation"
    when
        $rec : Recommendation($breed : breedName, $roleId : roleId)
        $g : ComputeBreedGoal(breedName == $breed, roleId == $roleId)
    then
        retract($g);
        System.out.println("[Backward] Retracted goal for " + $breed + " / " + $roleId + " after recommendation created.");
end
```

## queries.drl

```text
package com.github.cetkovicaleksa.droolmate.rules;
import com.github.cetkovicaleksa.droolmate.model.Breed;
import com.github.cetkovicaleksa.droolmate.model.RoleRequirement;
import com.github.cetkovicaleksa.droolmate.model.PhysicalScore;
import com.github.cetkovicaleksa.droolmate.model.BehavioralScore;
import com.github.cetkovicaleksa.droolmate.model.Recommendation;

dialect "mvel"

/**
 * Query definitions for the DroolMate expert system
 * Used to retrieve recommendations and scores from working memory
 */

// Query: Find all breeds in the system
query "all breeds"
    Breed()
end

// Query: Find all role requirements in the system
query "all roles"
    RoleRequirement()
end

// Query: Find all recommendations for a specific role
query "recommendations for role" (String roleIdParam)
    $rec : Recommendation(roleId == roleIdParam)
end

// Query: Find all recommended breeds (score >= 75)
query "recommended breeds"
    Recommendation(recommended == true)
end

// Query: Find top candidates for a specific role (ordered by score)
query "top candidates" (String roleIdParam)
    $rec : Recommendation(roleId == roleIdParam)
end

// Query: Find physical scores for a breed
query "physical scores" (String breedNameParam)
    $ps : PhysicalScore(breedName == breedNameParam)
end

// Query: Find behavioral scores for a breed
query "behavioral scores" (String breedNameParam)
    $bs : BehavioralScore(breedName == breedNameParam)
end

// Query: Find breeds suitable for a role (excellent or good recommendations)
query "suitable breeds" (String roleIdParam)
    $rec : Recommendation(roleId == roleIdParam,
                   (recommendationLevel == "Excellent" || recommendationLevel == "Good"))
end
```
